hypothesis: A coupled ensemble of M=4 heterogeneous GNN architectures (e.g., GCN, GIN, GatedGCN, PaiNN) trained with the ensemble-consensus SSL loss using labeled QM9 data plus a large out-of-domain unlabeled molecular corpus (e.g., ZINC) will yield individual member models whose held-out test MAE on QM9 targets is significantly lower (p < 0.05) than individual members from a homogeneous coupled ensemble of four identical architectures and than individual models from decoupled supervised ensembles.
